sourceMovie.title: After Everything
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto After Everything (2023) as a romance theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The romance and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Illusionist
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If After Everything clicked for you, The Illusionist (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after After Everything?
faq.answer: Try The Illusionist, The Vow, The Spectacular Now—each shares DNA with After Everything while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is After Everything?
faq.answer: After Everything (2023) is best described as Romance, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is After Everything part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "After Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
